_comment: Objection library — adapt responses to your specific product/service
_instructions: Replace PRODUCT, BENEFIT, METRIC with real values before deploying

objections.response: I completely understand. Before I let you go — can I ask what you're currently using to solve [pain_point]? I just want to make sure I'm not wasting your time.
objections.fallback_if_rejected_again: No problem at all. I'll remove you from my list. Have a great day!

objections.response: Totally fine. When's a better time — would tomorrow morning or Thursday afternoon work for a quick 10-minute call?

That's good to know. A lot of our clients were using [competitor] before switching. What would have to be true for you to consider an alternative — what's the one thing your current solution doesn't do well?

I hear you on cost. What if I told you most clients recover the investment in the first [timeframe]? Can I show you the numbers in a quick demo — if it doesn't make sense financially, I'll be the first to tell you.
Of course. What specifically do you need to think through? I want to make sure you have everything you need to make the right decision.
Happy to. I'll send it right after our call. But I want to make sure I send the right thing — what specific outcome are you most interested in: [option_a] or [option_b]?
Totally makes sense. Who would be the right person to include in a conversation? And is there anything I should know about how they prefer to evaluate new solutions?
Understood — budget timing matters. The reason I'm reaching out now is so that when [next quarter] comes, you already know if this is worth allocating budget to. Can I show you a 10-minute overview?
Fair question. [Company_name] saw [specific_metric] in [timeframe]. Would it help to talk to one of our clients directly? I can set that up.
I get it — there's a lot of hype and a lot of disappointment in the AI space right now. What specifically didn't work when you tried it before? I want to make sure we're solving the right problem.

bridge_phrases: Here's what I'm seeing with clients in your space:
Let me give you a different angle on that:
The reason most people feel that way initially is:
